title: Podman remote tries SELinux relabel of incorrect directory, unless given an absolute path
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman tries to relabel the incorrect directory when starting a container in remote mode, unless given an absolute path to the volume.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Set up a container with podman remote access. 
2. Enter the container.
3. `mkdir test && cd test`
4. `podman run -it -v .:/foo:z busybox sh`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: error preparing container d41d9adeaad2a3984d1a181a7fec432172a3836d3736632dfd764705f20b90f2 for attach: SELinux relabeling of /var/home/user is not allowed
```

Note that podman is trying to relabel my whole home directory, not just `/var/home/user/test` as expected. 

Works correctly if I pass the absolute path of my volume instead of a relative one: `podman run -it -v $(pwd):/foo:z busybox sh`

**Describe the results you expected:**

`-v .:/foo` and `-v $(pwd):/foo` to be equivalent in remote mode.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Affects only podman --remote. 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.0
API Version:  4.1.0

Git Commit:   e4b03902052294d4f342a185bb54702ed5bed8b1

```

comments.body:
Very strange. Does the . directory end up being correct if you do not specify :Z, IE does

podman run -it --privileged -v .:/foo busybox ls /foo

End up with your homedir or with the test directory?
@vrothberg @ashley-cui @umohnani8 This looks like a good one for an intern.

> Very strange. Does the . directory end up being correct if you do not specify :Z, IE does
> 
> podman run -it --privileged -v .:/foo busybox ls /foo
> 
> End up with your homedir or with the test directory?

That lists my home directory, even if my cwd is somewhere else, so that definitely is incorrect behaviour.
So it doesn't seem to be strictly SELinux-related, but specifying a volume with a relative path is completely broken in remote podman, which sounds to me like a major bug.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Still a valid bug. In remote mode podman volumes mount the wrong directory, which, again, sounds like a major bug.

Seems to be fixed in podman 4.2.
